goal: Lexicon port: promote scratch/lexicon to src/cli/slop/_lexical/_words.py and rewire the four group-B kernels (imposters, sprawl, slackers, confusion) to consume Lexeme/Lexicon. Group-A rules (verbosity, cowards, tautology, hammers, stutter) keep using split_identifier directly. Validate against snapshot dogfood and the live test suite; capture before/after deltas in observation 04.

summary.title: Lexicon port: Lexeme/Lexicon promoted to live tree, group-B kernels rewired
summary.what: Two commits on feature/lexicon-port. (1) Promoted scratch/lexicon/ scaffolding to src/cli/slop/_lexical/_words.py (single module: Lexeme frozen dataclass, Lexicon scope-agnostic bag with per-exclude cached filtered views, UNIVERSAL_NOISE constant) plus src/tests/test_lexicon.py (43 tests). Refactored imposters, sprawl, slackers to consume Lexeme/Lexicon. confusion unchanged (downstream of imposters' profile labels). Group-A rules (verbosity, cowards, tautology, hammers, stutter) unchanged — they remain on direct split_identifier per the design (per-name analyses, no corpus query). Behaviour preserved: 402/402 tests pass; snapshot dogfood numbers identical (40 violations / 38 advisories / 52 waived; same per-rule breakdown). (2) Added UNIVERSAL_NOISE filtering to imposters' modal-token analysis: lex.modal_tokens(k=3, exclude=UNIVERSAL_NOISE) and lex.overlap(member, modal, exclude=UNIVERSAL_NOISE). Test suite still 402/402; dogfood numbers still identical. The Newman-14 + identifier-glue noise set does not concentrate enough in slop's own clusters to flip any cluster's profile_label on this corpus — filtering is a precision-of-internal-metrics improvement here, not a verdict-changing one.
summary.why: The scaffolding had earned its place in the package: 43 tests on the substrate validated the API surface, four group-B kernel inline usages mapped cleanly to Lexicon methods, and the ad-hoc Counter / modal / alphabet logic across kernels was the duplication the abstraction was designed to retire. Adding UNIVERSAL_NOISE filtering grounds imposters' modal-token analysis in literature (Newman et al. SANER 2017) instead of leaving generic words like 'id', 'result', 'next', 'key' to dominate cluster modal sets and inflate modal_overlap_mean. The conservative scope (filter applied to imposters only, not sprawl/slackers' affix-pattern detection) leaves the alphabet-detection semantic question open for separate measurement on a corpus where it actually matters.
summary.user_goal: Lexicon port: promote scratch/lexicon to src/cli/slop/_lexical/_words.py and rewire the four group-B kernels (imposters, sprawl, slackers, confusion) to consume Lexeme/Lexicon. Group-A rules (verbosity, cowards, tautology, hammers, stutter) keep using split_identifier directly. Validate against snapshot dogfood and the live test suite; capture before/after deltas in observation 04.

decisions.chose: Two-step commit: pure refactor first, then UNIVERSAL_NOISE addition
_build_affix_patterns signature changed from list[(name, file, line, tokens)] tuples to list[Lexeme]
UNIVERSAL_NOISE applied to imposters' modal-token analysis only, NOT to sprawl/slackers' affix-pattern detection
scratch/ directory deleted in the same commit as promotion

architectural_claims: Lexeme + Lexicon are now the canonical substrate for the four group-B lexical rules (imposters, sprawl, slackers, confusion); group-A rules (verbosity, cowards, tautology, hammers, stutter) remain on direct split_identifier per the design.
Filtering is a per-query, per-rule choice via Lexicon.method(exclude=...). The Lexicon contract is pure measurement; defaults apply no filter.
UNIVERSAL_NOISE (Newman 14 + identifier glue) is the literature-grounded floor; SE-boilerplate (manager/helper/etc.) is deliberately excluded from this set because it is lexical.hammers's job.
behavioral_changes: Snapshot dogfood numbers identical to baseline before this branch: 40 violations, 38 advisories, 52 waived across 28 rules; same per-rule breakdown. The pure refactor preserves behaviour by construction; the UNIVERSAL_NOISE addition does not flip any cluster verdict on slop's own corpus.
imposters.modal_overlap_mean values are now computed with Newman-14 + glue stripped from the modal-token set. Numerical values move; profile_label boundaries unchanged on this corpus.
migration_notes: The scratch/lexicon/ directory was deleted in commit 1c50216 alongside the promotion. Anything depending on its imports (none in the live tree) would break.

Future work extending UNIVERSAL_NOISE to sprawl/slackers' affix-pattern detection should be its own branch; the alphabet semantics question needs measurement on a multi-language corpus, not just slop's own snapshot.
